perturbation.original_text: iii) "Restricted Area" means any state, territory, province or other jurisdiction of the United States of America or Canada in which the Company or the Company's 1 <PAGE> Affiliates engage in or solicit business, or plan to engage in or solicit business (as evidenced by business records existing at the time of termination if known to Shareholder), at any time during Shareholder's employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates. The Restricted Area is deemed to include, at a minimum, the States of Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky and Wisconsin.
perturbation.changed_text: iii) "Restricted Area" means the location of the Company's headquarters. The Restricted Area is deemed to include, at a minimum, the States of Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky and Wisconsin.
perturbation.explanation: The change in the definition of "Restricted Area" in Section 1(a)(iii) creates ambiguity and conflicts with the specific list of states. The initial part of the definition is restricting the area to only the location of the Company's headquarters. This conflicts with the subsequent sentence, which specifies a list of states as the minimum area, creating uncertainty about which geographic areas are actually restricted. This contradicts the scope of the non-compete agreement in Section 1(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) IN GENERAL. As a consequence of Shareholder's prior ownership and operation of Unitel, Inc. and employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, Shareholder has and will receive and deal with confidential information and business methods which are the exclusive property of the Company and the Company's Affiliates, including, but not limited to, its financial data, market data, business practices, pricing techniques, market development techniques, acquisition and development plans, and relationships with customers and suppliers. Shareholder further acknowledges that the confidential information acquired from the Merged Companies or the Company or the Company's Affiliates are of such a value and nature as to make it reasonable and necessary for the protection of the Company and the Company's Affiliates that Shareholder not Compete with the Company or the Company's Affiliates within the area and for a period of time hereinafter set forth, and that the Company and the Company's Affiliates will be irreparably injured, and the value of their capital stock and goodwill irreparably damaged, if Shareholder were to use or disclose any of the confidential information concerning the Company or the Company's Affiliates which Shareholder has acquired or will acquire, or if Shareholder were to Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates. Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing restrictive covenant, Shareholder further represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that neither Shareholder nor any of Shareholder's Affiliates will, during the Restricted Period: (i) promote the business of any person or entity engaged in a business which Competes with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; (ii) solicit, divert or take away or attempt to solicit, divert or take away any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliate's customers, distributors, suppliers or patronage; (iii) attempt to seek or cause any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates clients or customers to refrain from patronizing the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; or (iv) employ or engage or attempt to employ or engage in any capacity any person employed or contracted by the Company or any of the Company's 2 <PAGE> Affiliates at the date of termination of Shareholder's employment with the Company and any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "Restricted Area" to the company's headquarters, which contradicts the subsequent sentence specifying a list of states (Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky, and Wisconsin) as the minimum area. This creates uncertainty about which geographic areas are actually restricted. Section 1(b) relies on a clear definition of "Restricted Area" for the non-compete clause, and this ambiguity directly conflicts with that section's intention to restrict the Shareholder from competing within a defined geographic region, thus satisfying the conditions for an 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: ii) "Compete" means to manage, operate, control, or participate in, or have any ownership interest in, or make loans to, or guaranty loans for, or act as surety for, or aid or advise as an employee, officer, director, consultant, agent or otherwise, whether directly or indirectly, any business (whether an individual, sole proprietorship, partnership, corporation, firm, joint venture, trust, or other entity) which is engaged in any business in which the Company or the Company's Affiliates is engaged.
perturbation.changed_text: ii) "Compete" means to manage, operate, or control a business that directly mirrors the Company's core services, using proprietary information obtained during Shareholder's employment.
perturbation.explanation: The change to the definition of "Compete" in Section 1(a)(ii) narrows its scope significantly. Originally, "Compete" included a wide range of activities, including indirect involvement with competing businesses. The modified definition restricts it only to directly managing, operating, or controlling a business that mirrors the Company's core services and uses proprietary information. This is an In-text contradiction to the original intent of preventing any form of competition. This is contradictory to the general purpose of the non-compete agreement described in Section 1(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) IN GENERAL. As a consequence of Shareholder's prior ownership and operation of Unitel, Inc. and employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, Shareholder has and will receive and deal with confidential information and business methods which are the exclusive property of the Company and the Company's Affiliates, including, but not limited to, its financial data, market data, business practices, pricing techniques, market development techniques, acquisition and development plans, and relationships with customers and suppliers. Shareholder further acknowledges that the confidential information acquired from the Merged Companies or the Company or the Company's Affiliates are of such a value and nature as to make it reasonable and necessary for the protection of the Company and the Company's Affiliates that Shareholder not Compete with the Company or the Company's Affiliates within the area and for a period of time hereinafter set forth, and that the Company and the Company's Affiliates will be irreparably injured, and the value of their capital stock and goodwill irreparably damaged, if Shareholder were to use or disclose any of the confidential information concerning the Company or the Company's Affiliates which Shareholder has acquired or will acquire, or if Shareholder were to Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates. Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing restrictive covenant, Shareholder further represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that neither Shareholder nor any of Shareholder's Affiliates will, during the Restricted Period: (i) promote the business of any person or entity engaged in a business which Competes with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; (ii) solicit, divert or take away or attempt to solicit, divert or take away any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliate's customers, distributors, suppliers or patronage; (iii) attempt to seek or cause any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates clients or customers to refrain from patronizing the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; or (iv) employ or engage or attempt to employ or engage in any capacity any person employed or contracted by the Company or any of the Company's 2 <PAGE> Affiliates at the date of termination of Shareholder's employment with the Company and any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Compete" significantly narrows its scope, focusing only on directly managing, operating, or controlling a business that mirrors the Company's core services and uses proprietary information. This contradicts the original intent, as evidenced by Section 1(b), which aims to prevent any form of competition to protect the Company's confidential information, business methods, and relationships. The original text included a wide range of activities, including indirect involvement with competing businesses, while the changed text focuses on only direct competition using proprietary information. Thus, the new definition limits what is considered competition and therefore contradicts the broader intention and the specific restrictions listed in the contradicted text, representing an In-Text Contradiction based on the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: a) DEFINITIONS. The following terms will have the meanings set forth below:
perturbation.changed_text: a) DEFINITIONS. The following terms may have the meanings set forth below:
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 1(a) from "will have" to "may have" introduces ambiguity regarding whether the definitions provided are mandatory or optional. This creates an in-text contradiction with all subsequent sections that rely on these definitions, as it's now unclear if the definitions are binding. This directly impacts the enforceability of Section 1(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) IN GENERAL. As a consequence of Shareholder's prior ownership and operation of Unitel, Inc. and employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, Shareholder has and will receive and deal with confidential information and business methods which are the exclusive property of the Company and the Company's Affiliates, including, but not limited to, its financial data, market data, business practices, pricing techniques, market development techniques, acquisition and development plans, and relationships with customers and suppliers. Shareholder further acknowledges that the confidential information acquired from the Merged Companies or the Company or the Company's Affiliates are of such a value and nature as to make it reasonable and necessary for the protection of the Company and the Company's Affiliates that Shareholder not Compete with the Company or the Company's Affiliates within the area and for a period of time hereinafter set forth, and that the Company and the Company's Affiliates will be irreparably injured, and the value of their capital stock and goodwill irreparably damaged, if Shareholder were to use or disclose any of the confidential information concerning the Company or the Company's Affiliates which Shareholder has acquired or will acquire, or if Shareholder were to Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates. Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing restrictive covenant, Shareholder further represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that neither Shareholder nor any of Shareholder's Affiliates will, during the Restricted Period: (i) promote the business of any person or entity engaged in a business which Competes with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; (ii) solicit, divert or take away or attempt to solicit, divert or take away any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliate's customers, distributors, suppliers or patronage; (iii) attempt to seek or cause any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates clients or customers to refrain from patronizing the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; or (iv) employ or engage or attempt to employ or engage in any capacity any person employed or contracted by the Company or any of the Company's 2 <PAGE> Affiliates at the date of termination of Shareholder's employment with the Company and any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 1(a) states that the terms 'will have' the meanings set forth, making the definitions mandatory. The changed text modifies this to 'may have', introducing ambiguity and making the definitions optional. This directly contradicts Section 1(b), which relies on these definitions for understanding key terms like 'Unitel, Inc.', 'Company', 'Affiliates', 'Shareholder', 'Restricted Period', and 'Restricted Area'. If the definitions are not mandatory, the enforceability of the non-compete agreement in Section 1(b) is undermined, creating a strong in-text contradiction, as described in the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: b) EXCEPTIONS. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this provision does not apply to the extent, and only to the extent, that such information is clearly obtainable in the public domain through no fault of Shareholder or Shareholder's Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: b) EXCEPTIONS. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this provision does not apply if such information is obtainable in the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: The deletion of the phrase "through no fault of Shareholder or Shareholder's Affiliates" from Section 2(b) creates a contradiction. The original clause specified that confidential information was protected unless publicly available due to external factors. By removing this, any information, even if leaked by the Shareholder, could potentially be considered exempt. This directly weakens and contradicts the non-disclosure agreement established in Section 2(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL. From the date hereof, Shareholder will maintain in strict confidence and will not, directly or indirectly, or through Shareholder's Affiliates, divulge, transmit, publish, release, or otherwise use of cause to be used in any manner contrary to the interests of the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, any confidential information relating to the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates' systems, operations, products, services, business methods, management practices, contracts, computer programs and data bases, records, development data and reports, quality control specifications, cost analyses, flow charts, know-how, consumer lists, supplier lists, prospects, market development programs, acquisition programs, personnel data, or any information relating to customers, suppliers, products, sales, acquisitions, acquisition plans, financial structure, or pricing, and other information of like nature. Shareholder acknowledges that all information regarding the Company or the Company's Affiliates compiled or obtained by, or furnished to Shareholder regarding the Company, the Company's Affiliates or their respective businesses is confidential information and the Company's and the Company's Affiliates' exclusive property. Upon demand by the Company, and in any event upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2(b) provided an exception to non-disclosure only if the information became public through no fault of the Shareholder. The changed text removes this critical condition, meaning that even if the Shareholder leaks the confidential information, it would now be considered an exception to the non-disclosure agreement. This directly contradicts Section 2(a), which mandates strict confidentiality and prohibits the Shareholder from divulging confidential information. The changed text weakens the non-disclosure obligation and makes it unenforceable if the Shareholder is the source of the leak, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: d) GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement will be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of Ohio. Non-exclusive venue and jurisdiction for any action arising hereunder will be in the Courts of Common Pleas of Cuyahoga County, Ohio or in federal courts situated in the Northern District of Ohio (Eastern Division), in addition to any other courts having venue and jurisdiction. Each party irrevocably consents to the personal and subject matter jurisdiction of said courts.
perturbation.changed_text: d) GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the state where the Shareholder resides at the time a dispute arises. Non-exclusive venue and jurisdiction for any action arising hereunder will be in any court that has jurisdiction over the matter.
perturbation.explanation: The change to Section 5(d) alters the governing law and jurisdiction. The original clause specified Ohio law and courts. The modified version states the governing law is the state of Shareholder's residence at the time of dispute, and jurisdiction is any court with jurisdiction. This introduces a substantial change to the contract's interpretation and enforcement. Section 5(e) binding effect assumes Ohio law, therefore, this is an in-text contradiction to Section 5(e).
perturbation.contradicted_text: e) BINDING EFFECT. This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Company, its successors and assigns, and Seller, and Seller's heirs, representatives and permitted assigns. The terms of this Agreement, as they relate to the Company's Affiliates, will also inure to the benefit of the Company's Affiliates, their successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Ohio law for governing the agreement. The changed text alters this, stating the law is dependent on the Shareholder's residence at the time of dispute, and the jurisdiction is any court with jurisdiction. This directly contradicts Section 5(e), which assumes Ohio law for the binding effect of the agreement. This change introduces a strong in-text contradiction according to the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, making enforcement unclear.
